questions.question: Which of the following is the major factor that affects the decision about performing an audit in an organization?
questions.description: The purpose or objective of the audit determines whether an audit is required and what type of audit should be performed.

questions.question: Which of the following is the major factor affecting results of internal audits?
questions.description: Internal auditors may face bias or conflicts of interest due to relationships with employees, affecting objectivity.

questions.question: Which control classification attempts to repair the impact of a threat?
questions.description: Corrective controls restore systems or minimize damage after an incident has occurred.

questions.question: Define Internal Controls?
options: Control Tools used to detect risks
Ensures proper functioning of processes within the company
questions.description: Internal controls are policies and procedures designed to safeguard assets, ensure accuracy, and improve operational efficiency.

questions.question: Which is not an information security threat?
questions.description: Poverty is a social/economic issue, not an information security threat.

questions.question: What are the types of Internal Controls?
questions.description: Internal controls are commonly classified as Preventive, Detective, and Corrective/Reactive.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best way for an auditor to determine the effectiveness of a security awareness and training program?
options: Review the security reminders to employees
questions.description: Interviewing employees verifies whether the training has actually improved awareness.

questions.question: Reactive Internal Control is also called as
questions.description: Reactive controls respond after an incident and are also known as corrective controls.

questions.question: Logging all activities for later analysis is a
questions.description: Logs help detect suspicious events after they occur, making logging a detective control.

questions.question: As an auditor you visit an organization for auditing and observe following things. Which of the following can be a threat for the organization?
options: No important documents on employee desks
Old paper records dumped into UPS room with heat and dust
Visitor book collects only visitor name and mobile number without ID verification
questions.description: Lack of visitor identity verification poses a significant physical security threat.

questions.question: Which of the following is the ideal Audit team reporting structure?
options: CFO/CEO, IT Audit Committee, Director of Audits, Audit Manager, Audit Team
IT Audit Committee, Director of Audits, Audit Manager
questions.description: This structure provides independence, accountability, and proper reporting.

questions.question: Which are the following are roles of System Administrator?
options: Employing security persons to guard resources
questions.description: Maintaining system logs is a routine responsibility of a system administrator.

questions.question: ______ Internal Control does not prevent errors from occurring, but provides systematic way to detect and correct the situation.
questions.description: Detective controls identify errors so corrective actions can be taken.

questions.question: Which are the activities done during Audit Planning?
options: Confirmation of planned actions, Audit Response verification
Internal Control questionnaire, Audit scopes, Objectives
questions.description: Planning defines audit scope, objectives, and prepares questionnaires/checklists.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a Best Practice in securing a web server?
questions.description: Only necessary services should run to reduce the attack surface.

questions.question: A method of evaluating the security of a computer system or network by simulating an attack by a hacker is called as?
questions.description: Penetration testing simulates real attacks to identify vulnerabilities.

questions.question: As an auditor you require to check if the antivirus is installed on all the computers and is properly updated. There are almost 2000 computers. Which method will you choose?
options: Select some critical systems and some rarely used systems
questions.description: Audit sampling provides reasonable assurance without checking every system.

questions.question: Which data gathering technique is most important in auditing?
options: Review documents like policies and previous audit reports
questions.description: Documentation provides objective audit evidence.

questions.question: Software Change controls, access controls, backup and recovery are examples of
questions.description: These are mechanisms implemented to reduce risks.

questions.question: Identify the activities of Audit Assessment Phase.
questions.description: Assessment includes collecting evidence, evaluating controls, and testing them.

questions.question: Identify the outcome of Audit Planning Phase.
options: Meeting, Scope, Objectives, Questionnaire, Plan and Checklist
questions.description: Planning produces all documents needed before fieldwork begins.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a port scanner?
questions.description: `dig` is a DNS lookup utility, not a port scanner.

questions.question: Select the best option which describes Risk Management.
questions.description: Risk management includes identifying, analyzing, and treating risks.

questions.question: Windows Logs include:
questions.description: Windows Event Viewer stores Application, Security, and System logs.

questions.question: Audit Issue: Developers have direct access to update production code. Recommendation?
options: Allow DB management only to local admin
questions.description: Developers should not directly modify production systems without formal change management.

questions.question: If an Auditor wants to express an opinion, then he should have
questions.description: Audit opinions must be supported by sufficient and appropriate evidence.

questions.question: Which best defines Audit Sampling?
options: Applying audit procedures to less than 100% of items in a population
questions.description: Audit sampling evaluates a representative subset instead of every item.

questions.question: Audit Issue: Default umask is 000. Recommendation?
questions.description: `umask 027` provides secure default permissions.

questions.question: Buying insurance for a company is a
questions.description: Insurance transfers financial risk to another party.

questions.question: Which service helps to setup firewall in Linux?
questions.description: `iptables` is the traditional Linux firewall framework.

questions.question: Which is the basic Risk Management Process?
options: Risk Analysis, Develop Plan, Take Action, Measure Results
Risk Analysis, Develop Plan and Mitigate it
questions.description: This reflects the continuous risk management lifecycle.

questions.question: What is Event ID in Windows?
options: Uniquely identifiable events that Windows can encounter
questions.description: Every Windows event has a unique Event ID.

questions.question: When is the Audit Exit Meeting conducted?
questions.description: The exit meeting communicates audit findings before issuing the final report.

questions.question: Which is true about Qualitative Risk Analysis?
questions.description: Qualitative analysis categorizes risks without numerical calculations.

questions.question: Which is not true about Quantitative Risk Analysis?
questions.description: High/Medium/Low ratings belong to qualitative risk analysis.

questions.question: ISO 27001:2005 is a
questions.description: ISO/IEC 27001 is the international standard for Information Security Management Systems.

questions.question: According to ISO 27001, the most important factor for effective implementation of ISMS is
questions.description: Leadership commitment is essential for successful ISMS implementation.

questions.question: Which phase cycle approach is described by ISO 27001?
questions.description: ISO 27001 follows the PDCA continual improvement model.

questions.question: If replacement cost is $10,000 and probability of loss is 0.10, calculate Risk.
questions.description: Expected Risk = Asset Value x Probability = $1,000.

questions.question: Which tool allows parsing and querying logs using SQL statements?
questions.description: Microsoft Log Parser allows querying log files using SQL-like syntax.
